key_insight: CoTAR reconstructs molecular topology, formal charge, and unpaired electrons from condensed-phase MD trajectories by combining a GNN with HMM temporal smoothing and chemistry priors.
engineering_relevance: This enables bond-aware analysis and downstream classical-force-field workflows for universal ML interatomic potentials that otherwise lack explicit molecular structure.
method: Message passing on a proximity graph with a van der Waals prior
Chemical-constraint decoding for bonds, charges, and unpaired electrons
HMM smoothing to enforce temporal consistency across snapshots
evidence_signal: On 128 nonreactive condensed-phase systems, CoTAR achieved a bond-order-weighted F1 score of 0.906 on classical-MD data, and few-shot fine-tuning improved uMLIP valid-snapshot rate from 38.6% to 84.7%.
watch_item: Performance is reported for topology-preserving, nonreactive systems, so behavior on reactive chemistry or bond-breaking trajectories may be less reliable.
paper_title: CoTAR: Topology and Atomic State Reconstruction in Condensed Phases
